perturbation.original_text: The term "Evaluation Material" also shall be deemed to include all notes, analyses, compilations, studies, interpretations or other documents prepared by each party or its Representatives which contain, reflect or are based upon, in whole or in part, the information furnished to such party or its Representatives pursuant hereto which is not available to the general public.
perturbation.changed_text: The term "Evaluation Material" also shall be deemed to include all notes, analyses, compilations, studies, interpretations or other documents prepared by each party or its Representatives which contain, reflect or are based upon, in whole or in part, the information furnished to such party or its Representatives pursuant hereto, including information available to the general public.
perturbation.explanation: By modifying the definition of 'Evaluation Material' in Section 1 to include information available to the general public, it directly contradicts the original intent of the agreement. Previously, the definition excluded publicly available information, implying confidentiality. Now, by including it, Section 2's confidentiality obligation is undermined. This creates uncertainty about what exactly needs to be kept confidential and contradicts Section 1, which initially suggested 'Evaluation Material' was only private information. The phrase "which is not available to the general public" has been omited.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Each party hereby agrees that it and its Representatives shall use the other party's Evaluation Material solely for the purpose of evaluating, negotiating and consummating a possible transaction between the parties, and that the disclosing party's Evaluation Material will be kept confidential and each party and its Representatives will not disclose or use for purposes other than the evaluation, negotiation and consummation of a transaction any of the other party's Evaluation Material in any manner whatsoever; provided, however, that (i) the receiving party may make any disclosure of such information to which the disclosing party gives its prior written consent and (ii) any of such information may be disclosed to the receiving party's Representatives who need to know such information for the sole purpose of assisting the receiving party in evaluating, negotiating and consummating a possible transaction between the parties (it being understood that such Representatives shall be informed by the receiving party of the confidential nature of such information and shall be directed by the receiving party to treat such information confidentially). In any event, the receiving party shall be responsible for any breach of this Agreement by any of its Representatives and the receiving party agrees, at its sole expense, to take all reasonable measures (including but not limited to court proceedings) to restrain its Representatives from prohibited or unauthorized disclosure or use of the Evaluation Material.
perturbation.justification: The changed text modifies the definition of 'Evaluation Material' to include information available to the general public, which directly contradicts the original intent of the agreement to keep Evaluation Material confidential. The contradicted text states that 'Evaluation Material will be kept confidential,' but the change now includes publicly available information in the definition, creating an inconsistency. This undermines the confidentiality obligation in the contradicted text.

perturbation.original_text: Each party agrees that neither party nor any of its Representatives shall have any liability to the other party or to any of its Representatives relating to or resulting from the use of such other party's Evaluation Material or any errors therein or omissions therefrom.
perturbation.changed_text: Each party agrees that neither party nor any of its Representatives shall have any liability to the other party or to any of its Representatives relating to or resulting from the intentional misuse of such other party's Evaluation Material. However, liability will be held for errors or omissions therefrom.
perturbation.explanation: The original text in Section 6 absolves both parties of liability for errors or omissions. The modified text introduces liability for errors and omissions. This creates a contradiction regarding liability, specifically relating to mistakes within the Evaluation Material. The contradiction arises as the text now implies both no liability for intencional misuse but liability for errors.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Each party understands and acknowledges that neither party nor any of its Representatives makes any representation or warranty, express or implied, as to the accuracy or completeness of the Evaluation Material made available by it or to it. Each party agrees that neither party nor any of its Representatives shall have any liability to the other party or to any of its Representatives relating to or resulting from the intentional misuse of such other party's Evaluation Material. However, liability will be held for errors or omissions therefrom. Only those representations or warranties which are made in a final definitive agreement regarding the transaction, when, as and if executed, and subject to such limitations and restrictions as may be specified therein, will have any legal effect.
perturbation.justification: The original text states that neither party has liability for errors or omissions in the Evaluation Material. The changed text states that there is no liability for intentional misuse but introduces liability for errors and omissions. This directly contradicts the original statement of no liability for errors or omissions. The contradicted text, which is identical to the changed text, contains the same contradictory statements regarding liability for errors and omissions following the intentional misuse section. Therefore, there is a strong in-text contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: Each party also agrees that unless and until a final definitive agreement regarding a transaction between the parties has been executed and delivered, neither party will be under any legal obligation of any kind whatsoever with respect to such a transaction by virtue of this Agreement or otherwise except for the matters specifically agreed to herein.
perturbation.changed_text: Each party also agrees that unless and until a final definitive agreement regarding a transaction between the parties has been executed and delivered, both parties will be under legal obligation for every agreed terms in this agreement. It will be enforced immediately.
perturbation.explanation: In Section 9, the original text states that parties are not legally obligated until a final agreement is signed. This protects each party from being prematurely bound by the preliminary discussions and this agreement. The modified text creates in-text contradiction in the enforcement of the terms discussed in this agreement before the final agreement.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Each party understands and agrees that no contract or agreement providing for any transaction involving the parties shall be deemed to exist between the parties unless and until a final definitive agreement has been executed and delivered. Each party also agrees that unless and until a final definitive agreement regarding a transaction between the parties has been executed and delivered, both parties will be under legal obligation for every agreed terms in this agreement. It will be enforced immediately. For purposes of this paragraph, the term "definitive agreement" does not include an executed letter of intent or any other preliminary written agreement. Both parties further acknowledge and agree that each party reserves the right, in its sole discretion, to provide or not provide Evaluation Material to the receiving party hereunder, to reject any and all proposals made by the other party or any of its Representatives with regard to a transaction between the parties, and to terminate discussions and negotiations at any time.
perturbation.justification: The original text states that no legal obligation exists until a final agreement is signed. The changed text introduces an immediate legal obligation for agreed terms before the final agreement, directly contradicting the initial statement in both the original text and the contradicted text which indicates that no contract exists until a final definitive agreement has been executed. This creates a clear and direct conflict regarding when obligations begin, causing a strong in-text contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: In that case, or at any time upon the request of either disclosing party for any reason, each receiving party will promptly redeliver to the disclosing party all written Evaluation Material furnished to the receiving party or its Representatives by or on behalf of the disclosing party pursuant hereto, and will not retain any copies, extracts or other reproductions in whole or in part of such written material.
perturbation.changed_text: In that case, or at any time upon the request of either disclosing party only when reason is specified, each receiving party will promptly redeliver to the disclosing party all written Evaluation Material furnished to the receiving party or its Representatives by or on behalf of the disclosing party pursuant hereto, and will retain any copies, extracts or other reproductions in whole or in part of such written material.
perturbation.explanation: The original text in Section 5 allows the disclosing party to request the return of Evaluation Material at any time, for any reason, and prohibits the receiving party from keeping copies. The changed text contradicts that by specifying the disclosing party must specify reason and the receiving party is entitled to retain copies. This affects the process and control of the Evaluation Material within the text.
perturbation.contradicted_text: TERMINATION OF DISCUSSIONS. If either party decides that it does not wish to proceed with a transaction with the other party, the party so deciding will promptly inform the other party of that decision by giving written notice thereof. In that case, or at any time upon the request of either disclosing party only when reason is specified, each receiving party will promptly redeliver to the disclosing party all written Evaluation Material furnished to the receiving party or its Representatives by or on behalf of the disclosing party pursuant hereto, and will retain any copies, extracts or other reproductions in whole or in part of such written material. All documents, memoranda, notes and other writings whatsoever prepared by the receiving party or its Representatives which contain, reflect or are based upon the information in the Evaluation Material shall be destroyed or, at the receiving party's election, shall be delivered to outside legal counsel of the receiving party (where such materials shall be held in confidence, except as needed in the context of actual or threatened legal proceedings, or destroyed), and such destruction or delivery to outside counsel shall be certified in writing to the disclosing party by an authorized officer of the receiving party supervising such destruction or making such delivery to outside counsel.
perturbation.justification: The original text allowed the disclosing party to request the return of materials 'at any time for any reason' and prohibited the receiving party from keeping copies. The changed text now requires the disclosing party to specify a reason for the request and allows the receiving party to retain copies. The contradicted text references the changed text's new conditions regarding the specification of reason for return of materials and the retention of copies. This directly contradicts the original terms of the agreement, which would have allowed for an unconditional return of materials and a prohibition on retention. The change creates a direct and strong conflict, thereby satisfying the requirements for 'YES'.

perturbation.original_text: Notwithstanding the foregoing, the preceding restrictions contained in this paragraph 8 shall cease to be applicable if a third party shall formally commence any public tender or exchange offer for the outstanding common stock of the Company or if the Company shall publicly announce any proposed merger or other business combination involving the Company pursuant to which the outstanding common stock of the Company would cease to be listed on a national stock exchange.
perturbation.changed_text: Notwithstanding the foregoing, the preceding restrictions contained in this paragraph 8 shall cease to be applicable even if a third party shall formally commence any public tender or exchange offer for the outstanding common stock of the Company or if the Company shall publicly announce any proposed merger or other business combination involving the Company pursuant to which the outstanding common stock of the Company would cease to be listed on a national stock exchange.
perturbation.explanation: The original clause specified two conditions under which the standstill agreement would terminate. By replacing 'if' with 'even if', the conditions are still met with the third party public tender offer and merge, creating an in-text contradiction.
perturbation.contradicted_text: STANDSTILL. You agree that, during the period commencing the date hereof and ending on November 8, 2001, unless you shall have been specifically invited in writing by the Company, neither you nor any of your affiliates will in any manner, directly or indirectly, (a) effect or seek, offer or propose (whether publicly or otherwise) to effect, or cause or participate in or in any way assist any other person to effect or seek, offer or propose (whether publicly or otherwise) to effect or participate in (i) any acquisition of any securities (or beneficial ownership thereof) or assets of the Company or any of its subsidiaries; (ii) any tender or exchange offer, merger or other business combination involving the Company or any of its subsidiaries; (iii) any recapitalization, restructuring, liquidation, dissolution or other extraordinary transaction with respect to the Company or any of its subsidiaries; or (iv) any "solicitation" of "proxies" (as such terms are used in the proxy rules of the Securities and Exchange Commission) or consents to vote any voting securities of the Company; (b) form, join or in any way participate in a "group" (as defined under the Securities Exchange Act of 1934); (c) otherwise act, alone or in concert with others, to seek to control or influence the management, Board of Directors or policies of the Company; (d) take any action which might force the Company to make a public announcement regarding any of the types of matters set forth in (a) above; or (e) enter into any discussions or arrangements with any third party with respect to any of the foregoing. You also agree during such period not to request the Company (or its directors, officers, employees or agents), directly or indirectly, to amend or waive any provision of this paragraph (including this sentence). Notwithstanding the foregoing, the preceding restrictions contained in this paragraph 8 shall cease to be applicable even if a third party shall formally commence any public tender or exchange offer for the outstanding common stock of the Company or if the Company shall publicly announce any proposed merger or other business combination involving the Company pursuant to which the outstanding common stock of the Company would cease to be listed on a national stock exchange.
perturbation.justification: The original text stated the standstill agreement would cease to apply *if* certain conditions (third party tender offer or company merger announcement) were met. Changing 'if' to 'even if' in the changed text maintains that the agreement ceases to apply, but makes it broader and less conditional. The 'Contradicted Text' states the standstill agreement and all its restrictions. The changed text in paragraph 8 modifies this section to state that 'even if' the standstill is in effect, the agreement is not applicable given the criteria. This alteration makes the two clauses contradict. This falls under 'Structural Flaws - In Text Contradiction' because a clause is placed in multiple locations with conflicting implications. This creates uncertainty about the obligations and rights of each party.

perturbation.original_text: Each party understands and acknowledges that neither party nor any of its Representatives makes any representation or warranty, express or implied, as to the accuracy or completeness of the Evaluation Material made available by it or to it.
perturbation.changed_text: Each party understands and acknowledges that both party and its Representatives makes representation and warranty, express or implied, as to the accuracy or completeness of the Evaluation Material made available by it or to it.
perturbation.explanation: This perturbation makes the parties liable for misrepresentation, in contradiction to the original clause. This modification makes the parties now responsible for the accuracy and completeness of the information shared.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Each party understands and acknowledges that both party and its Representatives makes representation and warranty, express or implied, as to the accuracy or completeness of the Evaluation Material made available by it or to it. Each party agrees that neither party nor any of its Representatives shall have any liability to the other party or to any of its Representatives relating to or resulting from the use of such other party's Evaluation Material or any errors therein or omissions therefrom. Only those representations or warranties which are made in a final definitive agreement regarding the transaction, when, as and if executed, and subject to such limitations and restrictions as may be specified therein, will have any legal effect.
perturbation.justification: The original text states that neither party makes any representation or warranty about the accuracy of the evaluation material. The changed text directly contradicts this by stating that both parties make representation and warranty about the accuracy and completeness of the Evaluation Material. The contradicted text reiterates that both party and its Representatives makes representation and warranty, express or implied, as to the accuracy or completeness of the Evaluation Material. Therefore, the modified text creates a direct conflict, fitting the definition of 'Structural Flaws - In Text Contradiction' because the clause is placed in multiple locations with conflicting implications.
